_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn to select, use and reflect on both general and context-specific learning strategies.

_source.w: We learn to select, use and reflect on both general and context-specific learning strategies.
y: Strategic learners deliberately choose and adapt their approaches based on the task at hand.
Reflecting on our strategies helps us identify what works and continuously improve.
This self-regulated learning is what distinguishes successful students and professionals.
eg: We select learning strategies based on the demands of specific tasks and subjects.
We monitor the effectiveness of our chosen strategy and switch if it is not working.
We reflect after completing a task on which strategies worked and why.
We build a personal toolkit of strategies and know when to deploy each one.
We adapt general strategies like note-taking for specific contexts like science versus history.
We set learning goals and select strategies that will help us achieve them.
